title: SetupManifest
description: Agentic installer manifest. Declares platform matrix, prerequisites, params, and validated script steps for reproducible agent-driven installation.

apiVersion.description: API version for the setup framework.

install_type.description: Audience and scope of this manifest. 'user' = production deploy. 'developer' = local dev environment. 'ci' = headless pipeline setup.

platforms.description: Supported platform targets. Installer refuses to run on unlisted platforms.

params.description: User-facing parameters resolved before any step executes.

prerequisites.description: Prerequisite checks run before any step. Required ones abort if missing.

os_config.description: OS-level configuration entries. Developer manifests only. Each entry declares a check and an apply command for OS mutations (kernel params, group membership, file permissions, shell profile changes).

recovery.description: Named recovery procedures referenced by steps via on_fail.

briefing.description: Messages delivered to the user on completion.

shell.description: Shell context. 'wsl2' = WSL2 bash on Windows. 'native' = PowerShell/cmd.

type.description: Value type. 'path' values are expanded (~ → home dir).

default.description: Default value if not provided by user.

interactive_required.description: If true, the installer must prompt for this param interactively. Cannot be pre-filled by default in developer manifests. Use for params where a wrong default would cause OS configuration changes (SSH key email, local domain, GPG key ID, preferred IDE).

detect.description: Shell command. Exit 0 = present. Non-zero = missing.

version_min.description: Minimum version string. Parsed from detect command stdout.

install_hint.description: Human-readable hint shown when prerequisite is missing.

id.description: Unique identifier. Referenced by os-config steps via config_id.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this config entry does.

check.description: Shell command. Exit 0 = already configured, skip apply. Non-zero = needs configuration.

apply.description: Shell command or multi-line script to apply the configuration.

requires_relogin.description: If true, installer must warn the user that a logout/login or new shell is required for this change to take effect.

interactive.description: If true, apply triggers a GUI dialog or interactive prompt. Installer must pause and inform the user before running.

platforms.description: Platform(s) this entry applies to. Omit to apply on all declared platforms.

id.description: Unique step identifier. Referenced by depends_on and on_fail.

type.description: Step execution type. 'os-config' applies a single os_config entry by ID (developer manifests only).

platform.description: Platform(s) this step runs on. Omit to run on all declared platforms.

config_id.description: ID of the os_config entry to apply. Required for type=os-config.

script.description: Path to script file relative to manifest. Required for type=script.

params.description: Param names to pass to the script as env vars.

verify.description: Verification command(s) run after script step. Exit 0 = success.

commands.description: Commands to run for type=verify step.

message.description: Question presented to user for type=ask step.

on_deny.description: Action when user denies an ask step.

on_fail.description: Recovery procedure ID to invoke on failure. 'recover' invokes the default recovery.

manifest.description: Path or URL to chained SetupManifest for type=chain step.

depends_on.description: Step IDs that must complete successfully before this step runs.

when.description: Condition expression. Step skipped if evaluates to false. References params by name.
